title: Best SIM Cards and Mobile Data Options for Tourists in China
metaDescription: Learn how to get a SIM card or eSIM for mobile data during your China trip. Practical tips for tourists.

sections.heading: h1
sections.content: Stay connected in China with a local SIM card or eSIM. These options offer convenient data for maps, translation apps, and keeping in touch with family and friends back home.

sections.heading: h2
sections.content: Purchase a SIM card from China's major carriers (China Mobile, China Unicom, China Telecom) at airport shops. Bring your passport for registration. Prepaid tourist plans are commonly available.

sections.heading: h3
sections.content: Consider a travel eSIM from global providers before you go. Ensure your phone is unlocked and supports eSIM. Public WiFi is also widespread in hotels, cafes, and shopping areas.

keyTakeaways: Buy a prepaid SIM card with passport at arrival airports for easy setup.
Download essential apps like WeChat and a VPN service before entering China.
Check if your phone supports eSIM for a convenient, travel-ready option.
